t_ch: 缇安……
t_en: Trianne...

t_ch: 好安静啊！泰坦都住在这么暗的地方吗？
t_en: It's really quiet in here! Do all Titans live in dark places like this?

t_ch: 咦？欧洛尼斯……
t_en: Huh? Oronyx...

t_ch: 不会吧…悬锋人？
t_en: No way... A Kremnoan?

t_ch: 他们是自发前来应战的？还是……
t_en: Did they come to fight on their own accord? Or...?

t_ch: 就快到了……
t_en: We're almost there...

t_ch: 可真是让人好等……
t_en: You sure know how to keep a man waiting...

t_ch: 你们再不赶来，这泰坦怕是要牺牲我了。
t_en: Any later, and I fear the Titan would have sacrificed me.

t_ch: 呵，纤弱的人子…保你在刀下留个全身，可比解明至理还费力哪。
t_en: Heh. Frail child of humanity... 'tis more effort keeping you hale and whole against that blade than it is in seeking the truth of the world.

t_ch: 人海战术…我们分而击之！
t_en: They're trying to overwhelm us with numbers... Split up and take them out!

t_ch: 呵呵，派头十足啊，「救世主」。
t_en: Oho, what a spirited display, "Deliverer."

t_ch: 老师，准备动手！我们快顶不住了！
t_en: Get ready, Prof! We can't hold out much longer!

t_ch: 别吵，我自有分寸！
t_en: Quiet! I know what I'm doing!

t_ch: 黑潮的走卒——朕，悬锋众军之王，前来做你的敌手。
t_en: Minion of the black tide, I, king of the Kremnoan legion, will be your opponent today.

t_ch: 久违了哪…尼卡多利。
t_en: Long time no see... Nikador.

t_ch: 呵，雕虫小技！
t_en: Hmph, such petty tricks!

t_ch: 我权赐你与悬锋众英灵，同享尸床的权利——
t_en: Rejoice, for I grant you the honor of sharing your final resting place with Kremnos' departed heroes!

t_ch: 虚荣…自毁的薪柴。
t_en: Vanity... leads to self-destruction.

t_ch: 也不过…沉没一座王朝而已。
t_en: I am but sending a dynasty to its end.

t_ch: 好身手！为祭典开场足以尽兴……
t_en: Well fought! That was a good opener to the ceremony!

t_ch: 继续，就用你我的厮杀飨宴{RUBY_B#尼卡多利}死去的尊神{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: Now, we honor the {RUBY_B#Nikador}fallen gods{RUBY_E#} with this fight to the death!

t_ch: 又来这套…万敌，我配合你进攻。
t_en: Not the same trick again... Mydei, I'll follow your lead.

t_ch: 不足为虑，放手搏杀便是！
t_en: This is nothing to be worried about. Just fight to your heart's content!

t_ch: …该结束了。
t_en: ...It's time to end this.

t_ch: 来吧！悬锋人的投矛一向利落——命运自会为我制导枪头！
t_en: Come! The Kremnoans' spear always finds their mark — Fate itself shall forge my speartip!

t_ch: 我们回来了，阿格莱雅。
t_en: We're back, Aglaea.

t_ch: 好消息我已知晓：吾师能安然归来，便是证明。
t_en: I already know the good news. Tribbie's safe return is proof of that.

t_ch: 你应当先和我商议一番的，白厄。
t_en: You should have come to me first, Phainon.

t_ch: 阿雅，还是不要苛责小白了…奥赫玛的处境，非常危险。
t_en: Agy, there's no need to scold Snowy... Okhema is currently in grave danger.

t_ch: 我知道。难以想象，人类唯二能仰赖的泰坦竟在如此短的时间内先后遭遇毒手…金线前所未有地紧绷，仿若一触即溃。
t_en: I know. It's beyond my imagination that the only two Titans humankind can still rely on fell in such quick succession... I've never seen the golden thread pulled this taut, as if it could snap at any moment.

t_ch: 白厄，你们的计划…时间紧迫，长话短说吧。
t_en: Phainon... Tell us your plan. Time is of the essence.

t_ch: 白厄转述了那刻夏的发现……
t_en: Phainon recounts Anaxa's discovery...

t_ch: 不仅是狩猎泰坦，还要剥夺它们的火种…？
t_en: This being is not just hunting Titans, but taking the Coreflames, too?

t_ch: 对，和「逐火之旅」如出一辙…那刻夏老师认为盗火行者的出现，也许是黑潮进一步蔓延的表征。
t_en: Yes, just like the Flame-Chase Journey is doing. Professor Anaxa thinks the Flame Reaver's arrival is likely a sign that the black tide has spread further.

t_ch: 且不论推论的真伪，它远比想象中更来势汹汹啊。
t_en: Regardless of whether you're right, this being is still far more aggressive than we expected.

t_ch: 但这股疯狂也是我们的机会：既然火种是它的目标，人类就能诱导它落入陷阱。
t_en: But its madness is also an opportunity for us. If its goal is the Coreflame, then that means us humans can lure it into a trap.

t_ch: 在我们前去重渊之时，那刻夏老师已带着{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种离开圣城，提前赶往战场布局。
t_en: Anaxa had already left the holy city with {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces'{RUBY_E#} Coreflame while we were on the way to the Abyss, so he can make preparations for the battlefield.

t_ch: 说说看，凭什么你们觉得——结局不会是又一颗火种的丢失？
t_en: Tell me, what makes you think this won't end with us losing another Coreflame?

t_ch: 因为这一次，天外的伟力同样站在我们身边……
t_en: Because this time, the power from beyond the sky is also at our side...

t_ch: 关键在于{M#他}{F#她}。
t_en: {M#He}{F#She} is the key.

t_ch: 就知道，还得是我。
t_en: I knew it. Of course it's got to be me.

t_ch: 真的？我能做到吗…
t_en: Really? Are you sure I can do it?

t_ch: 我有一颗星核，能冒充火种吗？
t_en: I have a Stellaron. Can we pass it off as a Coreflame?

t_ch: 还需要你的另一位同伴。
t_en: We need your other companion too.
Oh...

t_ch: 难道…在说人家？
t_en: Do you mean... me?

t_ch: 原来如此。白厄，你方才提到的「战场」……
t_en: I see. Phainon, that "battlefield" you mentioned...

t_ch: 莫非是<color=#dbc291ff>悬锋城</color>？
t_en: Is it <color=#dbc291ff>Castrum Kremnos</color>?

t_ch: 正是。各位一定记得，{NICKNAME}和{M#他}{F#她}的伙伴在{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}一役中施展过令人惊叹的神迹，唤醒了悬锋城的过往。
t_en: Yes. I'm sure everyone remembers the astonishing miracle {NICKNAME} performed with {M#his}{F#her} companion during the battle with {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} and how they awakened Castrum Kremnos' past.

t_ch: 这便是致胜的关键——我们要重施故技，用{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的力量打开往日之门，诱敌深入……
t_en: That will be the key to our victory — We will use {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s power to once again open a passage to the past and lure our enemy inside...

t_ch: 然后，将盗火行者「封印」在岁月的迷宫。
t_en: Then, we'll "seal" the Flame Reaver in the Maze of Time.

t_ch: 不错，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}已经陨落，{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的神力也为奥赫玛所有。只要能夺回火种，那剑士便毫无逃离的手段。
t_en: Impressive. {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} has fallen, and {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}' divine power already belongs to Okhema. Once we take back the Coreflame, the swordmaster will have no means of escape.

t_ch: 并非裹血力战，而是以奇制胜——很聪明的做法。
t_en: It's very clever, using the element of surprise instead of brute strength.

t_ch: 小家伙，你能办到吗？
t_en: Can you do it, little one?

t_ch: 是说把黑色的家伙，丢到过去，再关起来吗？因为有记忆碎片，开启入口当然没问题，可剩下的……
t_en: I just have to throw that black-cloaked guy into the past, and lock them there, right? With the memory fragments, I can open a door no problem. As for the rest...

t_ch: 只能说——相信人家和{NICKNAME}一起，就什么都能做到吧！
t_en: Well, let's just say that {NICKNAME} and I can do anything as long as we're together!

t_ch: 没错，我们无所不能！
t_en: That's right! We can do anything!

t_ch: 是的！感受到大家热切的目光了吗？这么重大的场合，可不能让他们失望呀！
t_en: Yeah! Can you feel their fervent gazes? This is a big deal! We can't let everyone down!

t_ch: 是吗，我心里可没底…
t_en: Really? I'm not so sure about this...

t_ch: 拜托啦，感受到大家热烈的目光了吗？这么重大的场合，可不能让他们失望呀！
t_en: Aw, come on! Can't you feel their fervent gazes? This is a big deal! We can't let everyone down!

t_ch: 真的？那我可要许愿了——
t_en: Really? Then I'm gonna make a wish!

t_ch: 拜托啦，感受到大家热烈的目光了吗？这么重大的场合，可不能让他们失望呀！
t_en: Aw, come on! Can't you feel their fervent gazes? This is a big deal! We can't let everyone down!

t_ch: 很激动的样子呢…看来是颇有信心。
t_en: Such enthusiasm. I'm glad to see you're confident.

t_ch: 几位有所不知：吾师失踪的同时，元老院也已告急。凯妮斯一派还是得知了树庭的事，暗中开始造势，恐怕在下一次公民大会前就会有所行动。
t_en: By the way, the Council of Elders declared a state of emergency when Tribbie went missing. Caenis and her following have heard tell of the incident at the Grove and are galvanizing support in secret. I fear she'll make her move before the next assembly of the citizens.

t_ch: 她们打算做什么……
t_en: What are they plotting...

t_ch: 目光短浅的野心家，大概是觉得黄金裔的利用价值将尽，准备提前布局吧？总有一天，我们也必须面对「人」的问题。
t_en: That short-sighted politician likely believes the Chrysos Heirs have outlived their usefulness and is making preparations for her next step. That ugly side of humanity is something we'll have to deal with eventually.

t_ch: 但…不是现在。我已派遐蝶赶往元老院，捎去口信。有她在，足以暂时稳住局势。
t_en: ...But that's a problem for another day. I've sent Castorice ahead to the Council with a message. She should be able to keep the situation under control for the time being.

t_ch: 白厄，我希望与你达成共识：我们要将那盗火行者一举拿下，斩获战果。此后，面对元老院的挑战，我们也将有自己的底牌。
t_en: Phainon, it is my hope that we can come to an understanding on this: We must take down the Flame Reaver in one go and retrieve the Coreflame, and in doing so, grant ourselves an advantage against the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 当然，我愿意打头阵。
t_en: Of course. You may count on me to lead the charge.

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}阁下，如今奥赫玛内忧外患，又将你推上浪尖。
t_en: I apologize for tasking you to spearhead the plan once again, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, but Okhema is currently beset by enemies from within and without.

t_ch: 但事态紧急，实在没有与你细细磋商的余地…此事核心在你，我尊重你的选择。
t_en: The situation brooks no delay, and we have precious little time to confer and discuss the plan with you... That being said, you are the key to our success, and I shall respect any decision you make.

t_ch: 如何保证计划的可行性？
t_en: How do we make sure that the plan will work?

t_ch: 虽然对「百界门」的掌握不如缇安，但只要各位能争取些时间，*我*也可以开启一扇漂亮的门。
t_en: I may not be as masterful as Trianne when it comes to the Century Gate, but even I can open a nice, big gate if I have enough time.

t_ch: 至于能否夺得火种，就端看那刻夏的能耐了。
t_en: As for whether we can take back the Coreflame... that will be up to Anaxa's capabilities.

t_ch: 以我对那个人的认识…他一定能做到。
t_en: Based on what I know of him, he can do it.

t_ch: 怎么保证我的安全？
t_en: How do we guarantee my safety?

t_ch: 坦率地说，我们无法保证这个计划的安全性……
t_en: I'll be honest — we can't.

t_ch: 但…盗火行者的剑想要伤到你，得先穿透我的胸膛。
t_en: But the Flame Reaver will have to step over my dead body if they wish to do you harm.

t_ch: 迷迷的计划就是我的计划。
t_en: Whatever Mem's plan is, I'll follow.

t_ch: 太好啦！人家还怕你生迷迷的气呢。
t_en: Hooray! I was so worried that you'd be mad at me.

t_ch: 我们保留随时抽身的权利。
t_en: We reserve the right to withdraw at any time.

t_ch: 自然。与盗火行者交锋后，若阁下心力不支，自可抽身脱离战场，我们另寻他法。
t_en: Of course. Should you feel fatigued after our confrontation with the Flame Reaver, you are free to withdraw from the battlefield. We will find another way.

t_ch: 感谢阁下慷慨相助，也再次感谢各位。立刻开拔，围猎敌人吧——
t_en: We are grateful to you for your aid, and to the rest of you as well. Now, let us be off to begin the hunt.

t_ch: 愿{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}使你我眸光锋锐，克敌制胜。
t_en: May {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} sharpen our gaze and grant us victory over the enemy.

t_ch: 是不是少了个人？
t_en: Feels like we're missing someone.

t_ch: 你说万敌么？他正在解决另一桩难题呢。
t_en: You mean Mydei? He's dealing with a different problem.

t_ch: 放心，那个男人是绝不会缺席这场战役的。
t_en: Don't worry. That man would never miss out on this fight.

t_ch: 一旦夺回火种，{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的门径就会开启。大家必须马上逃离，绝不能停留。
t_en: Once we take back the Coreflame, {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}' passage will open. When that happens, you must evacuate immediately. Don't stay behind.

t_ch: 剩下的…就看*我们*的默契啦。
t_en: The rest... will be up to how well we work together.

t_ch: 在你们踏入战场前，我有一事想托付给你。
t_en: Before you step into battle, there's something I wish to ask of you.

t_ch: 请替我照看白厄，人面对仇敌时，总会叫愤恨迷了双目，连沉重的命运都难以看清。
t_en: Please, look after Phainon for me. Humans are often so blinded by hate when their enemy is before them, thus have trouble seeing even the heavy weight of destiny.

t_ch: 有我在，别担心。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll be by his side.

t_ch: 我试试，不一定拦得住他…
t_en: I'll try, but I don't know if I can stop him...

t_ch: 他视力挺好啊，我相信白厄。
t_en: He can see just fine. I have faith in Phainon.

t_ch: 感谢你。愿你们荣归，战场的骄子。
t_en: You have my thanks. May you return in glory, elites of the battlefield.

t_ch: 奥赫玛 创世涡心
t_en: Vortex of Genesis, Okhema

t_ch: 别了，挚友。你一定要…带领我等还乡……
t_en: Farewell, my dear friend. You must... lead us back home...

t_ch: 都一样…你和我…我们都把纷争和恐惧当做食粮……
t_en: You and I, we're both the same... We feast on strife and fear...

t_ch: 你若真能带领族人打破这染血的命运，就再次证明给我看吧，迈德漠斯。
t_en: If you're truly capable of leading our people beyond this bloodstained fate, then prove it to me once more, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 你果然在这里。
t_en: I knew you'd be here.

t_ch: 我听说了，你们马上就要远征。
t_en: I heard you're about to set off on an expedition.

t_ch: 没错，这会是无比艰难的一仗。目的地…还是悬锋城。
t_en: That's right. It's going to be a tough battle, and our destination is... turns out to be Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 所以，你是来向死去的{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}祈祷的？
t_en: Is that why you're here? To pray to the dead {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 不，恰恰相反。我来是为了告诉你……
t_en: No, just the opposite. I'm here to tell you...

t_ch: 这一次，你只管把前线交给我们。你将要面对的战场…同样不容失利。
t_en: You can entrust the front line to us this time. You can't afford to lose your upcoming battle either.
Hmph...

t_ch: 「开山者」吉奥刻勒斯——他的故事，你听过么？
t_en: Have you heard the story of Geocles the Mountainbreaker?

t_ch: 看来你和哈托努斯聊过了啊。这个名字，是那位传说中的山之民吧？
t_en: Sounds like you've spoken with Chartonus. That's the name of the legendary Mountain Dweller, right?

t_ch: 分别前，再多说几句吧。
t_en: Let's have a chat before we go our separate ways.

t_ch: 哈托努斯告诉我，远古，山之民们都隐居在山丘中。他们终日挖掘、采集…过着平和质朴的生活，远离纷争。
t_en: Chartonus told me that back in ancient times, the Mountain Dwellers lived in seclusion. They spent their days quarrying and gathering, living a simple and peaceful life away from strife.

t_ch: 但吉奥刻勒斯是个异类，他不认可传统，向往山外的世界。温吞的习俗，在他眼中仿佛一座监牢。
t_en: But Geocles was different. He resisted their traditional way of life, yearning for the world beyond the mountain. Their safe but humdrum culture was like fetters around him.

t_ch: 和你的处境很像啊。
t_en: He sounds a lot like you.

t_ch: 没错，所以他也必须面对同样的抉择：某日，吉奥刻勒斯一如往常，翻出山丘。但他看见，山脚下驻扎了一整支拉冬人的营队。
t_en: Yes, that is why he, too, faced the same dilemma as I do. One day, during one of Geocles' many ventures beyond the mountain, he spied a battalion of Ladonians stationed at the foot of the mountain.

t_ch: 那群拉冬人正和宿敌坎帕人交战，把山岳当成了游击的本营。吉奥刻勒斯自学了通用语，他从士兵口中套出了情报——
t_en: The Ladonians were at war with their archenemies, the Campanians, and they used the mountain as a base to carry out guerrilla warfare operations. When Geocles, who had taught himself the common tongue, approached a soldier, he learned a shocking piece of news:

t_ch: ——拉冬人计划在山间布满暗雷和炸药，并诱敌深入。那代表山之民的家园将不再安全，从此会危机四伏。吉奥刻勒斯即刻返回村落，将消息告知族人。然而……
t_en: The Ladonians were planning to plant mines and explosives all over the mountain, and then lure their enemies within. With danger lurking with every step, the Mountain Dwellers' homes were no longer safe. Geocles quickly returned to his village to bring the news to his people, but...

t_ch: 我猜猜…他们宁愿忍耐，也不愿求变？
t_en: Let me guess: They refused change and chose to put up with the situation instead?

t_ch: …没错。山之民畏惧改变，更遑论迁徙。战争将至，长老却只打算求和，不愿离开山里。吉奥刻勒斯感到悲哀，愤怒。
t_en: ...That's right. The Mountain Dwellers were fearful of even the smallest change to their lifestyle, let alone leaving their home. Despite the imminent war, the elders would rather negotiate for peace instead of leaving the mountains. This made Geocles both angry and sad.

t_ch: 但他并未放弃。若你是他，会怎么做？
t_en: But he didn't give up. If you were in his shoes, what would you have done?

t_ch: 带领族人发起反抗？说不上来，一时想不到什么两全其美的办法。
t_en: Lead my people on a resistance, perhaps? I'm not sure. I can't think of a solution that would make everyone happy.

t_ch: 「两全其美」——这正是你我的天真之处。而吉奥刻勒斯…我告诉你他是如何做的。
t_en: "A solution that would make everyone happy," huh... That's how naive we are. Well, let me tell you what Geocles did.

t_ch: 他拿走了山之民囤积的宝石，找拉冬人交换了大量火药。但不是为了抗击坎帕人——趁着夜色，他点燃聚落周围的山林，逼迫所有人逃出了山野。
t_en: He took the Mountain Dwellers' store of gemstones and traded them with the Ladonians for a massive amount of gunpowder — but he didn't do this to fight the Campanians. Under the cover of night, he set ablaze the forests surrounding the settlement, forcing his people to flee the mountain.

t_ch: 那场大火成了山之民历史的起点，自那之后，他们才汇入文明的大河，圣城中才出现了巨人的身影。而曾经的那片山岳，早已成了黑潮中的几点浮沫。
t_en: That great fire marked the beginning of Mountain Dweller history. Since then, their people became part of civilization, and giants became a common sight in the holy city. Meanwhile, that mountain they once lived in has turned into little more than foam inside the black tide.

t_ch: 但离开故土，也让许多山之民遭遇不幸。比如……
t_en: But leaving their homeland also brought many Mountain Dwellers misfortune. They —

t_ch: …被悬锋族人猎捕，或沦为奴仆。没错，那亦是历史的一部分。
t_en: They were hunted by Kremnoans or enslaved. Indeed, that is undeniably also part of their history.

t_ch: 这便是吉奥刻勒斯，他的功过自有人评说。但历史依然奉其为「开山者」，你觉得这是为何？
t_en: But that is Geocles for you. The merits of his actions — or the lack thereof — have been scrutinized by many, but historians nevertheless hail him as the Mountainbreaker. Why do you think that is?

t_ch: 这道理再直白不过——<color=#dbc291ff>因为他行动了，用自己的双手刻写下变革。</color>他没有试图让所有人理解自己，也从未在两全其美的困境中蹉跎。
t_en: It's simply because <color=#dbc291ff>he took action and enacted change with his own hands.</color> He didn't try to make everyone understand him, nor did he even waste time trying to make everyone happy.

t_ch: 他用一把大火烧毁了名为「传统」的枷锁，然后驱迫所有人…迈向滚滚而来的时代。
t_en: He started a great fire and burned the shackles of tradition to ashes, and then... forced everyone to march toward the coming of a new era.

t_ch: …你已下定决心了，是么？
t_en: ...You've made up your mind, haven't you?

t_ch: 不久之后，我就会离开奥赫玛，去和这世间的至暗搏斗，去背负…{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的命运。
t_en: I'll be leaving Okhema soon to fight the great darkness of this world... and to shoulder {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} destiny.

t_ch: 所以，仔细听好了——如果有一天，我们又在战场上相见，而我站在了逐火的对立面……
t_en: So, listen well: If there comes a day when we meet again on the battlefield, and I stand opposed to the flame-chase...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>记得从背后刺入我的第十节胸椎，那是唯一能杀死我的弱点。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Remember to stab your sword into my back through my tenth thoracic vertebra. That's my weak spot, and the only way to kill me.</color>

t_ch: 一言为定。
t_en: You have my word.

t_ch: 走吧，奔向你的战场，为那盗火的鬣狗送去死亡。
t_en: Go now. Your battlefield calls. Send that flame-thieving hyena to their death.

t_ch: 但，倘若是你们被逼上了绝路，那就向天上的锋刃祈祷——
t_en: But if you find yourselves with no other recourse, then pray to that blade in the sky...

t_ch: ——然后，大声呼唤新神的名字吧。
t_en: ...And loudly call out the name of the new god.

t_ch: 死神也抛弃了你啊，吾师。
t_en: I see the Reaper has forsaken you, too, teacher.

t_ch: 哼，它只是把我的性命交到了昏光庭院手中。
t_en: Hmph, it simply put my life in the hands of the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 那祭司姑娘的医术…不简单啊，她或许远没有你我想的单纯。
t_en: That priest girl's medical talents are truly exceptional... She might be far from the ordinary girl we envisioned.

t_ch: 但我的事不重要。迈德漠斯，我看到了…最后，你还是拥抱了自己的命运。
t_en: But this isn't about me, Mydeimos. I saw it... You've finally decided to embrace your own fate.

t_ch: 现在…「纷争」的力量在我胸中翻涌。我的筋骨化作钢铁，血液在沸腾燃烧。历代悬锋的王者只能仰望这股力量，但我——已经与它融为一体。
t_en: The power of Strife is stirring a tempest within me as we speak... My bones have turned into steel, and my blood is seething and ablaze. Past rulers of Kremnos could only aspire to this might, yet I have merged with it.

t_ch: 我缔造了历史。自我之后，神与王得以共生。
t_en: I've made history, and henceforth, divinity and kingship shall coexist as one.

t_ch: 没错，你实现了每一位悬锋先王的野心。
t_en: Indeed. You've realized the aspirations of every former king of Kremnos.

t_ch: 现在，你终于能带领族人还乡，去重铸悬锋的荣光了。
t_en: And now, you can at last bring our people home and reforge the glory of Kremnos.

t_ch: …可惜，我并无此意。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, I harbor no such desire.

t_ch: 这是…什么意思？
t_en: Wha—What do you mean by that?

t_ch: 两千余年前，崇拜{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}力量的人们聚集在它脚下，建立了城邦。悬锋人从此成了战神的锋刃，追随泰坦的步伐征讨四方。
t_en: Over two millennia past, devotees of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s might banded together beneath their feet and erected a city of their own. Ever since then, Kremnoans have served as the blades wielded by the god of war, following the Titan's path in its conquest.

t_ch: 但此刻，当我以「神」的身份重新俯瞰这段历史——我终于看清了一切。
t_en: Yet, gazing upon our history through the lens of my newfound divinity, I now see it with unprecedented clarity.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这是一段荒谬且卑微的历史。</color>人们如蝼蚁般奔赴战场、贪婪掠杀，也如蝼蚁一般…遭到践踏。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>This history is sheer absurdity and inferiority.</color> People surged onto the battlefield like ants, plundering and slaying for their own greed, only to be trampled upon... like ants.

t_ch: 悬锋人引以为傲的城邦、信仰，还有所谓的「传统」——在它眼中，不过是一触即溃的蚁穴。
t_en: The city and the beliefs that Kremnoans hold so dearly, alongside the supposed tradition... are mere anthills that can be crushed with a single touch in the eyes of the Titan.
You...!

t_ch: 你想用短短的几句话，剥夺悬锋族人累积千年的骄傲吗？！
t_en: Are you trying to strip Kremnoans of the pride we've amassed over thousands of years with mere words!?

t_ch: ——那骄傲不值一提，克拉特鲁斯。如今，我已接过「纷争」的神权。而接下来……
t_en: ...That pride holds little significance, Krateros. Now that I've assumed the divinity of Strife, my next step...

t_ch: 我将卸下，所谓「王」的名号。
t_en: Will be to renounce the title of "King."

t_ch: …不、不！
t_en: ...No! NO!

t_ch: 把我的话语传递给每一个悬锋人，我命令你。
t_en: Convey my message to every Kremnoan — this is an order.

t_ch: 别这么做，迈德漠斯……
t_en: Do not do this, Mydeimos...

t_ch: 我祈求你…！
t_en: I beg you...!

t_ch: 始于光历<unbreak>2506</unbreak>年，终于光历<unbreak>4931</unbreak>年。我，迈德漠斯，悬锋城的末代僭主，歌耳戈之子，在此宣告——
t_en: Our history commenced in Year <unbreak>2506</unbreak> of the Light Calendar and concluded in Year <unbreak>4931</unbreak>. I, Mydeimos, last King of Castrum Kremnos, son of Gorgo, do hereby proclaim...

t_ch: <size=28><color=#dbc291ff>于今日，悬锋王朝正式终结。</color></size>
t_en: <size=28><color=#dbc291ff>The Kremnoan dynasty comes to an official close today.</color></size>

t_ch: 你杀死了我们……
t_en: You have killed us...

t_ch: 你杀死了我们所有人……
t_en: You have killed us all...

t_ch: 不，我赐予了你们新生。
t_en: No, I am granting you a new beginning.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: What is this...

t_ch: 我母亲的印戒。它本是王者的证明，现在，我将赋予它崭新的意义。
t_en: My mother's signet ring. It served as a symbol of kingship, and I now imbue it with a fresh significance.

t_ch: 拿上它，召集失去了身份的人们。告诉他们，登上神阶的迈德漠斯昭告天下——
t_en: Take it and rally those who have lost their identities. Convey the words of Mydeimos, now ascended to divinity:

t_ch: 不必再去追求缥缈的荣誉，也不用再把战死当成唯一的归宿。但你们必须改变，融入这座曾以冷眼注视的城邦。
t_en: You no longer need to chase fleeting honors or face death on the battlefield as if it were your sole destination. However, you must adapt to fit into this city that you once observed with indifferent eyes.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯将迎来残酷的明天。大敌当前，再辉煌的王朝也不过是一堆瓦砾。
t_en: A cruel fate awaits Amphoreus. No matter how illustrious a dynasty may seem, it crumbles to rubble and gravel before formidable foes.

t_ch: 但你们会踏上唯一的出路，它的尽头将是崭新的世界。
t_en: Yet, you shall tread the only path out, one that leads to a brand-new world at its end.

t_ch: 为何要我做你的传声筒，为何你不去亲口向他们解释这一切？
t_en: Why task me with being your messenger? Why not explain all this to them yourself?

t_ch: 因为人要走的道路，只能由人来引领。不是觊觎神明之力，堕入疯狂的「人」……
t_en: Because only humans can guide the path to be trodden by humans. It's not the lot of "humans" who crave the power of gods and spiral into madness for it...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>而是愿意高举盾牌，保护身后生灵的「人」。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>But "humans" who stand ready to raise their shields high to protect the lives behind them.</color>

t_ch: 我没能救下那孩子。
t_en: I failed to save that child.

t_ch: 这不重要。同为半神，她们早已有所觉悟。
t_en: That is irrelevant. As demigods, they have long been prepared for it.

t_ch: 重要的是你会出现在那里——挡在她的面前。
t_en: What truly matters is that you showed up there and shielded her.

t_ch: 那一晚，说服阿格莱雅放了我的…是你么，迈德漠斯？
t_en: The one who persuaded Aglaea to set me free that night... Was that you, Mydeimos?

t_ch: 是我。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 她没有那么容易妥协。你开出了什么条件？
t_en: She's not one to easily make a concession. What did you offer her?

t_ch: 我押上了母亲的名誉，向她保证你灵魂的刚直。以及……
t_en: I staked my mother's name to guarantee the integrity of your soul to her, and...

t_ch: …我向她许下承诺，会将自己的一切，献予逐火的征途。
t_en: ...I promised her that I would dedicate all I have to the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 那现在呢，迈德漠斯？
t_en: And what is next, Mydeimos...?

t_ch: 你…要去往何处？
t_en: ...Where will you be headed now?

t_ch: 去履行我的诺言，回到我应回的地方。
t_en: To the place where I belong, to fulfill my promise.

t_ch: 那折磨{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的疯狂，吞噬整个翁法罗斯的黑暗……
t_en: The madness that has plagued {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} and the darkness that has consumed Amphoreus...

t_ch: 现在，轮到我与之抗争了。
t_en: It's now my turn to fight against it.

t_ch: 你来了，迈德漠斯……
t_en: You're here, Mydeimos...

t_ch: 看来你已做好准备。
t_en: It seems that you are prepared.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 你帮奥赫玛渡过了又一道难关，没有言语能表达我的谢意。
t_en: You aided Okhema through yet another trying ordeal, and my gratitude knows no bounds.

t_ch: 这不正是你想要的么？
t_en: Isn't that exactly what you wanted?

t_ch: 并非一切都在我的计划中。在如今的世道，我也只能步步为营。何况……
t_en: Not everything unfolds according to my plan. In the current state of the world, I can only proceed one step at a time. Furthermore...

t_ch: 即便没有对我的承诺，你也准备好献出自己的一切了，不是么？
t_en: You are ready to dedicate all that you have, regardless of the promise you made to me, aren't you?

t_ch: 在圣城的时间，我一直在向你学习：制定战略，领导众人，回应变数…但直到最近，我才看清了你真正令人诚服的特质。
t_en: Throughout my time in the holy city, I've gleaned valuable lessons from you — strategy, leadership, and adaptability to change... Yet, it's only recently that I've come to realize the truly remarkable quality you possess.

t_ch: 是什么？
t_en: And what is it?

t_ch: 你身上的「引力」。人们会自愿聚集在你的身边，聆听你的指引。
t_en: It's the magnetic allure in your presence. It draws people to gather around you willingly, to seek your guidance.

t_ch: 他们不想令你失望，并非出于对惩戒的恐惧，而是发自内心地认同你的理念。
t_en: They strive not to disappoint you, not due to fear of repercussions, but out of sincere alignment with your ideals.

t_ch: 那正是悬锋城的历代僭主缺失的东西。
t_en: That is precisely what every ruler of Castrum Kremnos has been missing.

t_ch: …你会这么想，我倍感荣幸。
t_en: ...I am truly honored to hear that.

t_ch: 你已经成了这世上最强大的半神，迈德漠斯。你打算如何使用这份力量？
t_en: And now, Mydeimos, you have risen as the most powerful demigod in the world. How do you intend to wield this newfound power?

t_ch: 我将完成{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}未竟的事业，成为翁法罗斯最坚固的墙垣，抵御黑潮的袭击。
t_en: I shall see {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s aborted mission through to completion, and become the strongest bulwark for Amphoreus to defend against the black tide.

t_ch: 我会为逐火之旅争取足够的时间，直到你带领所有人走到「创世」的奇迹面前。
t_en: I will secure enough time for the Flame-Chase Journey until you manage to lead everyone to reach the miracle of "Genesis."

t_ch: 翁法罗斯会迎来新生的，迈德漠斯…只是，带领人们抵达终点的不一定要是你我。
t_en: Mydeimos, a new chapter awaits Amphoreus... Yet, the one who guides the people to the destination need not be you and I.
Hehe...

t_ch: 接过{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}的神职后，你看见了什么？
t_en: What did you see after assuming {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia's{RUBY_E#} divinity?

t_ch: …即便过去了一千年，那道预言的字句仍旧清晰。
t_en: ...Despite the passage of a thousand years, every word of that prophecy remains as vivid as ever.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝将最后一次沐浴，在温热耀眼的黄金中。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall have your final bath in warm and radiant gold."</color>

t_ch: …呵，果然是不知所谓的末日预告。
t_en: ...Heh, just as cryptic as all the other prophecies.

t_ch: 哈哈……
t_en: Haha...

t_ch: 为何发笑？
t_en: What's the reason for the laughter?

t_ch: 我只是在想，换做常人看见这行字句，恐怕此生都不会再接近浴场。
t_en: I was just thinking... if an ordinary person encountered these words, they would probably stay away from the baths for the rest of their life.

t_ch: 但我戒不掉沐浴带来的宽畅享受。
t_en: But I cannot forgo the delightful experience a bath offers.

t_ch: 所以，你也看见了么，迈德漠斯？
t_en: So, I assume you also glimpsed yours, Mydeimos?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「终有一日，汝将背后负创而死。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"One day, you shall die with a wound in your back."</color>

t_ch: 还真是直截了当的画面啊。
t_en: Well, that is a rather straightforward scenario.

t_ch: 但如果我会被一则凭空出现的谵语吓破了胆，当初就不会受到你的注视，加入逐火的旅路。
t_en: If I were to be terrified by drivel with no basis, I would not have attracted your gaze and joined the Flame-Chase Journey in the first place.

t_ch: 你是对的。愿强有力的命运恭候你，也愿我们在承诺中的新世界再会……
t_en: You speak the truth. May you be destined for a powerful fate, and may we reunite in the promised new world...

t_ch: 再见了，万敌。
t_en: Farewell, Mydei.

t_ch: …万敌？
t_en: ...Mydei?

t_ch: 异乡人…不，开拓者们——我将动身前往悬锋城了。
t_en: Outlanders... no, Trailblazers, I will soon make my way to Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 假如万事顺利，我们也许不会再见。感谢你们为翁法罗斯带来的一切。
t_en: If all goes according to plan, this may be our final meeting. I just want to let you know that I appreciate everything you have done for Amphoreus.

t_ch: 重新审视过往，昂然步向未来，我也经历过这样的挣扎。
t_en: Reflecting on the past and moving forward to the future, a challenge I've faced as well.

t_ch: 虽未有机会与你交心，但共同经历了这么多…万敌，你同样是「开拓」的盟友。
t_en: Though we never had a chance for a deep conversation, after all we've experienced together... you are also an ally of the Trailblaze, Mydei.

t_ch: 这意义非凡。也祝你们的「开拓」无往不胜。
t_en: That means a lot to me. May success accompany your Trailblazing wherever it leads.

t_ch: 呵…你在想些什么，不如由我来猜猜？
t_en: Heh... How about you let me guess what's on your mind?

t_ch: 猜测：{F#她}{M#他}要说些鼓舞的话。
t_en: I guess... {F#She}{M#He} will say something inspiring.

t_ch: 在思考什么…鼓舞人心的话语么？
t_en: Are you thinking about... saying something inspiring?

t_ch: …多吃蔬菜，多锻炼；少看闲书，少熬夜。
t_en: ...Eat more greens, work out more often, avoid mindless reads, and get more sleep.

t_ch: 猜测：{F#她}{M#他}要说些煽情的话。
t_en: I guess... {F#She}{M#He} will say something sentimental.

t_ch: 莫非在酝酿什么…感人的道别辞？
t_en: Are you drafting... a sentimental farewell speech?

t_ch: …男孩子独自出门，要保护好自己。
t_en: ...Stay safe out there. A lone boy can get into a lot of trouble.

t_ch: 猜测：{F#她}{M#他}要说些胡话。
t_en: I guess... {F#She}{M#He} will say something nonsensical.

t_ch: 又要口出狂言了么，开拓者？
t_en: Are you going to say something insane again, Trailblazer?

t_ch: …没了你我们可怎么办啊，呜呜呜。
t_en: *sob* ...What are we going to do without you?

t_ch: 不知道该怎么回应的话，微笑就行了。
t_en: Just smile if you don't know how to respond.

t_ch: …哈哈。
t_en: ...Haha.

t_ch: 如今我身负神权，隐隐能感觉到…你们对翁法罗斯的未来重要非凡。
t_en: Now that I bear the divine within me, I can get the vague feeling that... that you have a great significance for the future of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 此去恐无归期。我能否将后背交予二位，恳请你们…继续协助逐火的征程？
t_en: With this departure, I fear that I will never again return. Can I entrust you both to watch my back... and to continue supporting the Flame-Chase Journey in my absence?

t_ch: …请你放心，我们会尽己所能。
t_en: ...Rest assured. We'll do everything we can.

t_ch: 感激不尽。再会了……
t_en: You have my gratitude. Then, farewell.

t_ch: …你，等等。
t_en: ...Hold on.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Yes?

t_ch: 合个影吧，至少留个纪念。
t_en: Let's at least snap a photo together to capture this moment, shall we?

t_ch: 哈…当然可以，来吧。
t_en: Hah... Sure. Let's do it.

t_ch: 感觉不错。你觉得呢，万敌？
t_en: Looks pretty good. What do you think, Mydei?

t_ch: 哼，怎么说呢…怪怪的？不是说拍照技术，是我很少给自己拍照……
t_en: Hmph, well... It feels peculiar, I suppose? I'm not talking about your photographic skills. It's just that I rarely take photos...

t_ch: 但，这张照片…真挺好的。
t_en: But this one... It's pretty good.

t_ch: 不愧是我。
t_en: I never fail to impress.

t_ch: 你能满意就好——别留遗憾。
t_en: I'm glad you like it. Make sure not to leave any regrets behind.

t_ch: …也祝你们在翁法罗斯不留遗憾。
t_en: ...I wish the same for you during your time in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 悬锋之王，虽无印戒…却更有王相，如今。
t_en: The King of Kremnos, without the signet ring... But looking more like a king now.

t_ch: 我已不是「王」了，大工匠。
t_en: I'm no longer the "king," Grand Craftsman.

t_ch: 并无所谓，称号。胜过所有旧王，你成就之事。
t_en: Matters not, the title. Surpassed all former kings, your achievements have.

t_ch: 我将担起你们的信任，令世界不再受恐惧主宰。
t_en: I will honor your trust and liberate the world from the clutches of fear.

t_ch: 但哈托努斯，请你继续关照白厄。我有一种预感，也许他的命运…比我们所有人加起来都更沉重。
t_en: Nevertheless, I must request that you continue to watch over Phainon, Chartonus. I have a feeling that his fate... will weigh heavier than all of ours combined.

t_ch: 白厄的事，我会记得。归乡去吧，勇敢的王。
t_en: Keep Phainon in mind, I will. Return to your home, brave king.

t_ch: 与众人一同，我将在此，聆听悬锋城…那道惊雷。
t_en: Together with everyone, I shall remain here. That thunder... from Castrum Kremnos, we wait to hear.

t_ch: 缇里西庇俄丝女士…不在此处么？
t_en: Is Lady Tribios not here...?

t_ch: 万敌阁下！
t_en: Lord Mydei!

t_ch: 风堇？
t_en: Hyacine?

t_ch: 原来克拉特鲁斯提到的医师是你，难怪他能取回一命。多谢。
t_en: So you are the physician Krateros spoke of. It's no wonder then that he was pulled back from the brink of death. You have my gratitude.

t_ch: 嘿…这是句夸奖，对吧？
t_en: Heh... I can take that as a compliment, right?

t_ch: 树庭的事，我听说了。请你节哀。
t_en: I've heard about what happened at the Grove. I am deeply sorry for your loss.

t_ch: …和死亡、悲伤有关的话，我最近已经听太多啦。用缇宝大人的话说，我们都该望向「明天」，不是吗？
t_en: ...I've heard far too much about death and grief recently. To quote Lady Tribbie, we should all focus on tomorrow, shouldn't we?

t_ch: 也对…你一直是我们当中最乐观的那个。
t_en: That's a valid point... You have always been the most optimistic one among us.

t_ch: 话说回来，目睹一位新的半神站在眼前…真是不可思议。
t_en: Well, I must say, it feels quite incredible... to see a new demigod standing right before me.

t_ch: 你也该做好准备了。
t_en: You should prepare yourself as well.

t_ch: ——「纷争」已被人类执握在手。终有一日，他们也能触及「天空」。
t_en: ...Humanity has already taken hold of "Strife," and one day, they will also reach the "Sky."

t_ch: …你觉得我能做到？毕竟连我的祖先，那位史诗中的英雄，都没能征服{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的火种。
t_en: ...Do you really believe I can do it? Even my ancestor, that hero of legends, couldn't conquer the Coreflame of {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你与那几位半神关系甚好，理应明白这个道理：夺取神权的手段，从来不止剑与矛一种。
t_en: Given your good rapport with the demigods, you should know that the path to assuming divinity is never limited to swords and spears.

t_ch: 总是这么一针见血呢，万敌阁下。请放心吧，假如命运真的指定了我，我会做好准备的。
t_en: You've always been so perceptive, Lord Mydei. Rest assured. If fate has truly selected me, I will be ready.

t_ch: 对了，你是来找缇宝和缇宁大人的吧？她们不在，有什么话需要我转述吗？
t_en: Right. You are here looking for Lady Tribbie and Lady Trinnon, no? They're not around at the moment. Is there a message you'd like me to pass on to them?

t_ch: 不必了。我已和阿格莱雅道过别，来见神谕者只是出于礼仪。
t_en: No need. I have already bid farewell to Aglaea. Meeting with the oracle is simply a matter of courtesy.

t_ch: 道…别？
t_en: You've bid... farewell?

t_ch: 我将返回悬锋城对抗黑潮，恐怕不会再回到奥赫玛了。
t_en: I'm heading back to Castrum Kremnos to fight against the black tide. In all likelihood, I will never again return to Okhema.
Oh...

t_ch: 如此说来，倒是有一句话想求你转述。
t_en: On second thought, there is something that I'd like you to help me convey to them.

t_ch: 尽管说吧，万敌阁下。我一定逐字逐句帮你传达。
t_en: I'm all ears, Lord Mydei. I'll make sure to convey every single word.

t_ch: ——告诉她们，以后不必再以肉身勘察敌后了。只需一条讯息，天谴的锋刃便会指向奥赫玛的敌人。
t_en: ...Tell them that there is no longer a need to send scouts behind enemy lines. With a single message, the Lance of Fury will target anyone who dares oppose Okhema.

t_ch: …万敌阁下。
t_en: ...Lord Mydei.

t_ch: 我先你一步走进自己的宿命了，遐蝶。
t_en: I'll be embracing my destiny before you do, Castorice.

t_ch: 你现在…能感受到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}吗？
t_en: Can you... feel {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} right now?

t_ch: 很可惜，我感受不到任何气息。
t_en: I don't feel them at all, regrettably.

t_ch: 如此漫长的岁月里，你一直在苦苦寻找它。但你从未想过找我做些尝试么？
t_en: You have been painstakingly seeking that Titan for so long, but have you ever considered reaching out to me for help?

t_ch: 你的意思是…？
t_en: You mean...?

t_ch: 死亡拒绝引渡我。若你将我送往冥界，或许我能在被遣返的路上找到些线索。
t_en: Death refused to ferry me. If you were to send me to the nether realm, I may uncover clues along the way back.

t_ch: 我不会那么做的，万敌阁下。死亡不是游戏。
t_en: I would never do that, Lord Mydei. Death is not a game.

t_ch: …你说得对。我的族人之所以挣扎千年，或许正是因为他们太过轻视死亡。
t_en: ...You're right. That may very well be the reason why my people have struggled for millennia — they underestimated the significance of death.

t_ch: 所以，你要独自返乡了吗，万敌阁下？
t_en: So, are you returning home alone, Lord Mydei?

t_ch: 还乡…也许吧。现在悬锋城不仅是我的故乡，还是我命运的归宿。
t_en: Returning home, you say... Heh, maybe. At this moment, Castrum Kremnos is not just my birthplace, but also my fated destination.

t_ch: 再努力些吧，遐蝶。去完成你必须完成的事，让你我这一路承受的磨难有所收获。
t_en: Work harder, Castorice. Go and do what you have to do, so that the trials we have endured may have meaning.

t_ch: 我会的，万敌阁下。
t_en: I will, Lord Mydei.

t_ch: 希望我们能于温暖西风的彼岸再会。
t_en: May we reunite on the other side, where the warm west wind blows.

t_ch: 哼，早该料到是你。
t_en: Hmph, I should've known you were the mastermind behind this.

t_ch: 可别随便冤枉人啊，这次真的与我无关。
t_en: Hey, don't go pointing fingers without cause. I swear I'm innocent this time.

t_ch: 看到那边的小孩子了吗？是他们把大家聚在一起的。
t_en: See those kids over there? They're the ones who brought everyone together.

t_ch: …那着实出人意料。
t_en: ...I admit, that took me by surprise.

t_ch: 莫非你真想就一走了之，都不跟我通个气？
t_en: Were you seriously just going to sneak off without a word with me?

t_ch: 我正打算来同你道别，半路就撞上了这番景象。
t_en: Well, I was actually on my way to bid farewell to you, but ended up running into this.

t_ch: 噢…那还真是荣幸。
t_en: Oh... That's quite an honor, I suppose.

t_ch: 最好还是别让他们等太久了，你说呢？
t_en: We better not leave them waiting too long, don't you think?

t_ch: 从这里到悬锋城，路途漫长。
t_en: The road to Castrum Kremnos from here is long.

t_ch: 多花些时间，最后一次看清他们的脸孔…未尝不可。
t_en: Perhaps it wouldn't be a bad idea... to take a moment and etch their faces in my memory one last time.

t_ch: 好了…就送到这里吧。
t_en: Alright... It's about time for us to part ways.

t_ch: 还有什么告别的话想说吗？这没准是最后的机会了。
t_en: Any final goodbyes you want to share? This might be your last chance.
Hmph...

t_ch: …怎么不出声了？我还以为神性会冲淡人的多愁善感。
t_en: ...Why so quiet? I thought divinity would wash away some of your sentiments.

t_ch: 你的心情果真如此轻松？亦或者…这又是你披上的伪装？
t_en: Do you truly feel as carefree as you seem? Or... is this just a facade you wear?

t_ch: …你现在的语气像极了阿格莱雅，知道吗？
t_en: ...You know what, you sound just like Aglaea at this moment.

t_ch: 不过…你没说错。我只是觉得，这种场合，强装轻松可能会显得更体面些。
t_en: But you're not off the mark... I just thought putting on a nonchalant front for this occasion might help us preserve some dignity.

t_ch: 我无需读心的能力，也能戳穿你的把戏。
t_en: I can see through your deception even without the power of mind-reading.

t_ch: 我的表情管理就这么差劲？
t_en: Am I really that terrible at controlling my facial expressions?

t_ch: 但话都说到这份上了…感谢你助我手刃仇敌，迈德漠斯。
t_en: Well, with that said... thank you for aiding me in defeating my archenemy, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 没必要感谢我。
t_en: No need to thank me.

t_ch: 这场胜利并无实感，也远非结束——你比我更清楚。
t_en: This triumph brings no delight, nor does it signify closure — you understand that more than me.

t_ch: …是啊。到头来，我依旧没能弄清那黑袍剑士的来历，甚至连它的死亡都无法确信。
t_en: ...Indeed. In the end, I still haven't unraveled the mystery behind that black-robed swordmaster, and I can't even ascertain their demise.

t_ch: 也许，我根本没有斩断自己的宿命。
t_en: Maybe I've never managed to cut off my destiny.

t_ch: 但那不重要。痛苦可以治愈，伤痕却不能，也不应被磨灭。即便借助{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的神力，过去也会在视线的死角留下蛛丝马迹。
t_en: But that matters not. Pain may be cured, but scars remain, and they should not be effaced. Even with the divine might of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}, you cannot stop the past from leaving its marks in our blind spots.

t_ch: 就算斩杀了那黑衣人，你心中的失落便能一笔勾销？不可能。复仇只是一件必须被完成的任务，但没有人能通过它得到心灵的满足。
t_en: Can you genuinely let go of everything as soon as that swordmaster is dead? That's impossible. Revenge is merely an obligation that must be carried out, and no one can find mental fulfillment through it.

t_ch: …谁说不是呢。
t_en: ...You're right.

t_ch: 向前看。把你的过往嚼碎、咽下，勿让空虚与苦涩成为你唯一熟知的滋味。
t_en: Look forward. Chew up and swallow your past, then make sure emptiness and bitterness are not the only flavors you know.

t_ch: 别忘了，你身后还有一整个世界等着被拯救。
t_en: Remember that there is a whole world behind you waiting to be saved.

t_ch: 我会铭记在心。
t_en: I will keep that in mind.

t_ch: 最后一个要求，代我照顾好悬锋的战士们。
t_en: I have a final favor to ask: Please look after the Kremnoan warriors in my absence.

t_ch: 融入奥赫玛的过程中，他们一定会遇到阻力…我唯一的遗憾，便是无法与他们同担这段时光。
t_en: They will undoubtedly face challenges while assimilating into Okhema... It is my sole regret that I cannot stand by them during this time.

t_ch: 放心，交给我吧。
t_en: Do not worry. Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我倒要找他们确认下，看看悬锋人的字典里是不是真的缺了那么多词语。
t_en: And while I'm at it, I'm going to find out if the Kremnoan language is really missing that many words.

t_ch: 哼，悬锋人的哲学从来不以字典彰显。
t_en: Hmph, the Kremnoan philosophy can never be encapsulated in a mere dictionary.

t_ch: 但…来世若有机会，来我的图书馆多看看吧。
t_en: But... if there's a chance in the next life, you should come visit my library.

t_ch: 走了，救世主——记得活到最后。
t_en: I'll be on my way, Deliverer. Remember to stay alive till the final act.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 你也是，别死了——
t_en: Same goes for you. Don't die too easily —

t_ch: 祝你战无不胜，迈德漠斯。
t_en: May triumph always be yours, Mydeimos.

t_ch: …对了。
t_en: ...Oh, right.

t_ch: 最后一个问题。
t_en: One last question.

t_ch: 问吧，别留遗憾。
t_en: Go ahead. You better not leave any regrets behind.

t_ch: 告诉哈托努斯那枚印戒存在的人，就是你吧？
t_en: Are you the one who told Chartonus about the signet ring?

t_ch: 这个嘛……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 谁知道呢？
t_en: Who knows?

t_ch: 哈…哈……
t_en: Hah... Hah...

t_ch: 站起来，孩子！你打得很勇敢，今天的训练就到这里。
t_en: Get up, my son! You showed great courage in today's training. Let's wrap it up for now.

t_ch: 是，母亲。
t_en: Yes, Mother.

t_ch: 母亲…我有一件事想问。
t_en: Mother... There's something I want to ask you.

t_ch: 怎么了，迈德漠斯？
t_en: What is it, Mydeimos?

t_ch: 为什么我们生来就要学习战斗？
t_en: Why must we learn to fight?

t_ch: 为了荣耀，孩子。悬锋人一出生便认得剑和矛，战场是我们的归宿……
t_en: It is for glory and honor, my son. For Kremnoans, mastery of swords and spears is ingrained from birth, and the battlefield is our destined end.

t_ch: 真是如此吗，母亲？
t_en: Is that really the way it is, Mother?

t_ch: 为什么这么问？
t_en: What makes you say so?

t_ch: 因为你听上去不太肯定。
t_en: Because you don't sound entirely sure.

t_ch: …你是对的，迈德漠斯。
t_en: ...You're right, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 我曾盲信那些字句，直到你的父亲将你抛入冥海。
t_en: I once believed those words without question until your father cast you into the Sea of Souls.

t_ch: 那时我才发现，自己信奉的一切都脆弱不堪。也许悬锋精神真的存在过…但随着贪欲之花绽放，它早已随我们的荣耀一同凋零了。
t_en: It was at that moment I realized how everything I believed in was utterly hollow. Perhaps the spirit of Kremnos had once existed... but as greed bloomed in the hearts of humanity over the years, such spirit had long faded alongside our glory.

t_ch: 我不再相信任何誓言或教条。现在，我的身份只剩下一个——
t_en: I no longer put my faith in any oath or doctrine. Now, I have just one role...

t_ch: 那就是你的母亲，迈德漠斯，你的守护者……
t_en: That of your mother, Mydeimos. Your guardian...

t_ch: …梦总会结束于此。返回悬锋城的路途也比我预想更久。
t_en: ...That dream always ends there. The journey back to Castrum Kremnos is proving to be longer than expected.

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯说，在我坠海后不久，母亲便向先王发起了角斗——她死于父亲的毒计，我与她素未谋面。
t_en: Krateros said that, shortly after my fall into the sea, my mother challenged my father to a duel — She died through Father's treachery, and I never saw her face.

t_ch: 但她会在每个夜晚出现，等候在朦胧的火光中。我始终在等待梦的后续，以为她一定还会留下更多嘱托，或者指引……
t_en: Yet each night, she would show up, waiting by the flickering firelight. I always anticipated the sequel to that dream, believing she had more words of wisdom or guidance to offer...

t_ch: 但现在…我似乎明白了。这便是她想对我表达的全部。
t_en: But now... I think I have finally come to understand. That is all she wished to tell me.

t_ch: 如今，我只剩下一个身份……
t_en: And now, I too am left with but one role...

t_ch: 光历<unbreak>4931</unbreak>年 平衡月\n「纷争」之泰坦，天谴之矛，尼卡多利陨落。悬锋之王迈德漠斯战胜试炼，新神诞生。
t_en: Year <unbreak>4931</unbreak> of the Light Calendar, Month of Balance\nNikador — the Strife Titan, Lance of Fury — fell. Mydeimos, king of Kremnos, triumphed over the trial, and the new god was born.

t_ch: 是日，悬锋千年王朝宣告终结。
t_en: On that day, the Kremnoan dynasty, which had lasted for a thousand years, came to an end.

t_ch: 出发前，还有些人要见。
t_en: There are some people to meet with before I leave.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 王子来了，快跑！
t_en: The prince is here. Run!

t_ch: 错啦！迈德漠斯殿下已经不是王子了…他是<color=#dbc291ff>翁法罗斯的守护者</color>！
t_en: Hold on! Lord Mydeimos isn't the prince anymore... He's now <color=#dbc291ff>the Guardian of Amphoreus</color>!

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 向翁法罗斯的守护者——致敬——！
t_en: We salute the Guardian of Amphoreus!

t_ch: 向您致敬。
t_en: We salute you.

t_ch: 愿您战无不胜。
t_en: May triumph always be yours.

t_ch: 荣耀伴您左右。
t_en: May glory be with you.

t_ch: 代我们还乡吧，守护者！
t_en: Return home on our behalf, Guardian!

t_ch: 我们誓死追随您。
t_en: We dedicate our lives to following you.

t_ch: 你永远都是我们的王。
t_en: You will forever be our king.

t_ch: 再见了…悬锋的拯救者。
t_en: Farewell... Savior of Kremnos.

t_ch: 跟从您的指引。
t_en: Enlighten us with your guidance.

t_ch: 荣耀伴您左右。
t_en: May glory be with you.

t_ch: 向您致敬。
t_en: We salute you.

t_ch: 愿您战无不胜。
t_en: May triumph always be yours.

t_ch: 荣耀伴您左右。
t_en: May glory be with you.

t_ch: 跟从您的指引。
t_en: Enlighten us with your guidance.

t_ch: 你永远都是我们的王。
t_en: You will forever be our king.

t_ch: 我们誓死追随您。
t_en: We dedicate our lives to following you.

t_ch: 愿你战无不胜，悬锋的王。
t_en: May triumph always be yours, King of Kremnos.

t_ch: 再见了，迈德漠斯！
t_en: Farewell, Mydeimos!

t_ch: 请为我们抵御黑潮，守护者…
t_en: Please fend off the black tide for us, Guardian...

t_ch: 荣耀属于你，歌耳戈之子！
t_en: Glory belongs to you, son of Gorgo!

t_ch: 我想…「纷争」的神也许该改名了。
t_en: I'm thinking... Maybe it's about time the god of "Strife" gets a new name.

t_ch: 「团结」的神，如何？或者…「存护」？这名字是丹恒告诉我的。
t_en: How about the god of "Solidarity"? Or... "Preservation"? Dan Heng mentioned that one to me.

t_ch: 我劝你远离任何需要起名的场合。
t_en: I recommend that you steer clear of any situation that calls for naming things.

t_ch: 哈…看来还是「纷争」更适合你。
t_en: Hah... I suppose "Strife" still fits you better.

t_ch: 那…旅途也该抵达终点了。
t_en: It is time for this journey to reach its end.

t_ch: 看啊，迈德漠斯回来了！
t_en: Look, Mydeimos is back!

t_ch: 新鲜的石榴汁，一起尝尝嘛？
t_en: We've got fresh pomegranate juice. Want to try some?

t_ch: 欢迎回来！
t_en: Welcome back!

t_ch: 快来！看看我新写的……
t_en: Say! Come and check out what I just wrote...

t_ch: 迈德漠斯！你刚训练回来吗？
t_en: Mydeimos! Did you just come back from training?

t_ch: 真是…久违了。
t_en: It's... been a while.

t_ch: 欢迎回家…迈德漠斯。
t_en: Welcome home... Mydeimos.

t_ch: 你也找到值得守护之物了吗？
t_en: Have you found something deserving of your protection as well?

t_ch: 荣耀属于你。
t_en: Glory belongs to you.

t_ch: 欢迎回来！
t_en: Welcome back!

t_ch: 又见面了，王子。
t_en: We meet again, Your Highness.

t_ch: 训练得如何？
t_en: How was your training?

t_ch: 气色不错啊。
t_en: You're looking well.

t_ch: 期待和你并肩作战！
t_en: I look forward to fighting beside you!

t_ch: 愿尼卡多利捍卫你。
t_en: May Nikador defend you.

t_ch: 我会在祭典上打败你！
t_en: I'm going to beat you at the festival!

t_ch: 杀声震天啊，这可不像那刻夏老师一个人能搞出来的动静。
t_en: That's the sound of an all-out war. Not the kind of ruckus Professor Anaxa can raise on his own.

t_ch: 加快脚步吧，战场就在前方。
t_en: Let's hurry. The battlefield is just ahead.

t_ch: 好久不见，「守护者」。
t_en: It's been a while, "Guardian."

t_ch: 这些人里…好像不止有悬锋一族。
t_en: I think I see... more than just the Kremnoans here.

t_ch: 你现在可是「翁法罗斯」的守护者。
t_en: You're now the Guardian of Amphoreus, after all.

t_ch: 再见了，守护者！
t_en: Farewell, Guardian!

t_ch: 向你致敬！
t_en: We salute you!

t_ch: 您是我们的希望！
t_en: You're our hope!

t_ch: 请一路小心。
t_en: Have a safe journey.

t_ch: 与此同时 云石天宫某处
t_en: Meanwhile, somewhere in Marmoreal Palace

t_ch: 真没想到她能派悬锋人来助阵。勇敢的战士，前线战况如何？
t_en: I didn't think she'd manage to send the Kremnoans to assist. Brave warrior, what's the situation on the front line?

t_ch: …奥赫玛人？你为什么在这里……
t_en: ...An Okheman? Why are you here...?

t_ch: 为什么…阿格莱雅没告诉你们吗？我们受命前来协助各位，围猎盗火行者。
t_en: What...? Didn't Aglaea tell you? We have been instructed to assist you in trapping the Flame Reaver.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅…天杀的僭主，我呸！
t_en: Aglaea... That vile tyrant! I spit on her name!

t_ch: 我们的王…就要回来了。如果是他，一定能把你们…那个黑衣的……
t_en: Our king... will be back soon... He'll be able to... deal with you... and that black-robed...

t_ch: 嘶哑的詈骂郁在他的喉头，迟迟不发。不多时，此人痉挛着，头颅垂落，魂息飘离肢体，向{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的府邸而去。
t_en: A hoarse curse lodged in his throat, refusing to escape. Moments later, he convulsed, his head drooping as his spirit departed his body, drifting towards the abode of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 悬锋人…果真和他们的王如出一辙。
t_en: ...The Kremnoans really are just like their king.

t_ch: 安息吧，愿你的荣光长存。
t_en: Rest in peace. May your glory last forever.

t_ch: 还记得之前万敌说的话，身在战场，就要抛却杂念……
t_en: But remember what Mydei said before? "Cast aside all distractions once you're on the battlefield"...

t_ch: 上吧，搭档——今日，命运会站在我们这边！
t_en: Let's go, partner! Today, destiny shall stand with us!

t_ch: 尼卡多利站在我们这边！
t_en: Nikador is with us!

t_ch: 别让我们的王蒙羞！
t_en: We won't bring dishonor to the king!

t_ch: 为了悬锋！为了王！
t_en: For Kremnos! For the king!

t_ch: 荣耀属于神盾旅！
t_en: Glory to the Godshield Brigade!

t_ch: 我还能…继续…
t_en: I can still... fight...

t_ch: 请你一定要当上王…
t_en: You must become king...

t_ch: 声音…好刺耳…
t_en: That sound... It pierces my ears...

t_ch: 我看不见了…
t_en: I can't see...

t_ch: 看，战士们归来了！
t_en: Look, the warriors have returned!

t_ch: 但…传言是真的吗？{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}陨落了？
t_en: But... are the rumors true? Has {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} fallen?

t_ch: 和元老院说的一样，大敌当前，黄金裔只会引发更多战乱，而不是考虑民众的安危……
t_en: It's as the Council of Elders said. We face a dangerous threat, yet the Chrysos Heirs are only inciting more chaos. They don't care about the people's safety...

t_ch: 「纷争」的火种，最终还是给了悬锋人啊……
t_en: They really gave the Coreflame of "Strife" to a Kremnoan at the end...

t_ch: …真是聒噪啊。
t_en: What a noisy crowd...

t_ch: 气氛…有些微妙。
t_en: This... doesn't feel right.

t_ch: 应该…是因为{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的离去吧。
t_en: It's probably because {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} is gone...

t_ch: 在世人眼中，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}一直是位慷慨的泰坦。它降下神迹，允许人们从过往中捞取便利……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} was a generous Titan in the eyes of the people. They graced us with miracles, allowing humanity to reap many a convenience from the past...

t_ch: 但他们不知道，那位泰坦的「慷慨」并非自愿。
t_en: What they don't know is that the Titan didn't do all of that of its own volition.

t_ch: …无论如何，泰坦之死已成定局。我们得归还{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的火种。
t_en: Regardless, the Titan has fallen, and we must surrender {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s Coreflame.

t_ch: 呵…这么快又要去见那女人了，看来属于我的劫难远未到头啊。
t_en: Heh... a meeting with that woman again so soon? I just can't catch a break.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师，我想…不如你先找个地方休息片刻？
t_en: Professor Anaxa, why don't you... find someplace to rest first?

t_ch: 眼下需要阿格莱雅定夺的事已经扎堆了，再让你们俩见面——说得直接些，难免会分散她的精力。
t_en: Matters that require Aglaea's attention are piling up. You two meeting again will — forgive my honesty — only distract her.

t_ch: 只是，你最好表现得…安分一些。你明白我的意思。
t_en: Just... try to stay on your best behavior, if you know what I mean.

t_ch: 这恐怕由不得我。打踏入圣城的那一刻起，我就已经在她的监牢里了。谁让我的脑袋里住下了一位泰坦呢？
t_en: That's not up to me, I'm afraid. I've been trapped in her prison the moment I set foot in this holy city. The price of having a Titan living in my head, I suppose.

t_ch: 放心吧，我不想自讨苦吃。
t_en: Don't worry. I don't want trouble either.

t_ch: 他和阿格莱雅就那么不对付？
t_en: He and Aglaea don't get along, huh?

t_ch: 只是一些理念上的差异……
t_en: They have... differences in ideology.

t_ch: 好吧，这么说是有些轻描淡写了。别担心，你迟早会搞明白这些人际关系的。
t_en: Alright, I suppose that's putting it lightly. Don't worry, you'll figure out the intricacies of these relationships sooner or later.

t_ch: 这位老师脾气挺怪。
t_en: That prof's kinda eccentric.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师只是有些…桀骜不驯。别担心，你会习惯的。
t_en: Professor Anaxa can be a bit... Unrestrained. Don't worry. You'll get used to it.

t_ch: 处置{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的火种，想必不需要我在场。先告辞了，我还有些私事要处理。
t_en: I'm sure you don't need my help handling {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s Coreflame, so I'll be off. I have some personal matters to attend to.

t_ch: 小敌…总觉得完成试炼后，他的背影变得更孤单了。
t_en: It somehow feels like De has closed himself off even more after he completed his trial.

t_ch: 是啊。这短短几日，真是发生了好多事……
t_en: Yeah. A lot has happened in the past few days...

t_ch: 缇宝老师，从刚才开始，你也一副沉思的样子，是缇宁捎来了什么消息么？
t_en: You've been lost in thought for a while now too, Tribbie. Did Trinnon bring news?

t_ch: 嗯…她找着缇安了。
t_en: Yes... She has found Trianne.

t_ch: …在神殿外的山壑下。可惜，是个坏消息。
t_en: ...She was at the bottom of the valley outside the temple. But... that's where the good news ends.

t_ch: …请节哀。
t_en: ...I'm sorry.

t_ch: 没关系。不过除了缇安，现场还发现了另一个人……
t_en: It's alright. They also found someone else besides Trianne.

t_ch: 缇宁和风堇赶到的时候，那人已经和尸体无异了。他承受千刀万剐，又跳下万丈深崖，硬是将缇安的身躯带离了黑衣剑士的魔爪。
t_en: He was no better than a corpse when Trinnon and Hyacine arrived. He had suffered more cuts than could be counted and leaped off a cliff from a great height, all to free Trianne's body from the clutches of the black-robed swordmaster.

t_ch: 竟然还有这等勇士？能辨识出他的身份吗？
t_en: What a brave soul. Do we know who he is?
Yes...

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯。
t_en: It's Krateros.

t_ch: 不久前……
t_en: Not long ago...

t_ch: ——住手！
t_en: ...Stop!

t_ch: 呵。
t_en: Heh.

t_ch: 想取这女孩的性命？先跨越我的尸体。
t_en: If you want this girl dead, you'll have to kill me first.

t_ch: 可笑…血肉之躯，能接下哪怕一击？
t_en: How ludicrous... Your body, made of flesh and blood as it is, will not survive more than a single strike from me.

t_ch: 「宁战死，毋荣归」——我生而为悬锋人，何时惧怕过死亡？
t_en: "Valorous Death Before Glorious Return." As a Kremnoan, I know not the fear of death.

t_ch: 失去神力的空壳，我没兴趣……
t_en: She's lost her divinity. I have no interest in an empty shell.

t_ch: 但回答我，纷争之子，缘何，为她架起盾牌？
t_en: But answer me this, child of Strife. Why do you raise your shield for her?
Hmph...

t_ch: 我身为神盾旅的冠军勇士之首，曾手持这面圆盾，在黑潮面前为国王保驾护航，千千万万次……
t_en: As the leader of the Godshield Brigade's elite, I protected the king against the black tide with this very shield. And I've done this over and over, hundreds and thousands of times...

t_ch: 可悲的屠夫…你又怎会明白它的重量？
t_en: A pathetic killer like you could never understand the weight it bears.

t_ch: 可惜。
t_en: Pity.

t_ch: 那就如锈铁般，溃灭吧。
t_en: Then crumble like rusted iron.

t_ch: 求之不得！让我享用后代的奠酒和熏烟，享用至高无上的荣光！
t_en: Good! Supreme glory shall be mine as I indulge in the libations and incense offered by my descendants!

t_ch: ——来吧！为了悬锋，为了吾王……
t_en: Come, let us fight! For Kremnos! For my king!

t_ch: …为了翁法罗斯！
t_en: ...For Amphoreus!

t_ch: 我们回来了，阿雅。
t_en: We're back, Agy.

t_ch: 缇安…我们找到的时候，她已经变成这样了。
t_en: Trianne was already like this when we found her...

t_ch: 我们在千年前便已预见到离别的残酷，今日不过又品尝了一次它的滋味。
t_en: We foresaw this cruel parting a thousand years ago. Today is but a reminder of that bitter taste.

t_ch: 但是，阿格莱雅大人…我不明白，拖垮缇安大人的事物并不是生命……
t_en: But Lady Aglaea, I don't understand... It wasn't life itself that Lady Trianne lost...

t_ch: 她好像…真的变成了一只娃娃？我不懂……
t_en: Why does she look like she really turned into a doll? I... I don't understand...

t_ch: 别失落，风堇。缇安只是用完了从{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}那儿借来的时间。
t_en: Don't be sad, Hyacine. Trianne has used up her borrowed time from {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, that's all.
